There was another positive that warrants this rating as well, which is that on Thursday CBS News reported that President Donald Trump signaled plans to issue an executive disorder that would allow the potential to increase the research happening around ibogaine [a traditional psychedelic plant native to Africa]. Besides Definium being one of the many psychedelic beneficiaries of this move, other stocks that traded higher in tandem were Compass Pathways (CMPS), Atai Life Sciences (ATAI), GH Research (GHRS) and Cybin (HELP). This is bullish, and the hope is that many of these psychedelic companies can benefit. However, the report appears to note the need to look at developing ibogaine specifically for brain issues for, veterans, along with post-traumatic stress disorder [PTSD].

Financials

According to the 10-K SEC filing, Definium Therapeutics had cash, cash equivalents, and investments of $411.6 million as of December 31, 2025. The reason for the cash on hand is because back on October 29, 2025, it entered into an underwriting agreement in connection with an underwritten public offering of common stock. This October 2025 offering involved Jefferies LLC, Leerink Partners LLC, and Evercore Group L.L.C. as well. It was able to raise a total of $242.8 million after expenses. This was thanks to it selling a total of 18,375,000 shares of its common stock at a price of $12.25 per share. Along with the fact that it granted the underwriters an option to purchase up to an additional 2,756,250 shares of common stock at the very same price. Of which, the underwriters exercised their option in full.

The truth is that this company has plenty of cash runway. Why is that? That's because it believes that its cash on hand is enough to fund its operations into 2028. Barring some unforeseen cash raise to come about [if it releases positive data causing the stock price to trade higher], then it is in good shape. Especially since it also has another financial tool at its disposal, which is a sales agreement established back on June 28, 2024, with Leerink Partners LLC. The reason for this was so that the company could from time to time offer and sell up to $150 million worth of common shares. The good thing is that this company has not yet tapped into this sales agreement. As of December 31, 2025, no shares have been sold under it.

Risks To Business

There are several risks to consider before investing in Definium Therapeutics. The first risk to consider would be in terms of the first expected data readout. This relates to the release of topline data from the phase 3 Emerge study using DT120 ODT for the treatment of patients with MDD, expected in late Q2 of 2026. The risk here is that there is no guarantee that the primary endpoint of change from baseline in the Montgomery-Asberg Depression Rating Scale [MADRS] total score at week 6 will be met in a statistically significant manner. In addition, this is only the first phase 3 trial for this program. The second phase 3 study, to be known as Ascend, is not expected to have the first patient dosed until early Q2 of 2026. The risk here is that even if one study ends up being successful, there is no guarantee that a similar or superior outcome will be achieved by the other.

The second risk to consider would be in terms of the other program that this company is working on, which is the evaluation of DT120 ODT for the treatment of patients with GAD in the ongoing phase 3 Voyage and Panorama studies. The company expects to release data readouts from both of these studies in early Q3 of 2026 and the 2nd half of 2026, respectively. The company was able to initiate this late-stage trial program because it achieved durable, clinically meaningful, statistically significant data when it came to both the HAM-A and MADRS scales evaluating DT120 ODT over a 12-week period. The risk here is that there is no assurance that the statistical significance achieved in prior testing will yet again be replicated in either of these two ongoing phase 3 studies.

The third and final risk to consider would be the program I went over above, which is the use of another MDMA, or R(-)-MDMA DT402, for the treatment of patients with ASD in the ongoing phase 2a study. The company was able to boast an excellent safety profile along with substantial PK/PD data in a phase 1 healthy volunteer study that it allowed to move on to the phase 2a study. The risk here is that, first and foremost, there is no guarantee that there won't be any safety issues observed in the ongoing study. Secondly, the second study is intended to provide more detail into the use of this drug with respect to functional biomarkers. Even though the safety profile might remain intact, there is no guarantee that the functional biomarker data is going to end up being positive upon the data being released in 2026. Nor that such clinical data is going to be enough to allow the company to move this program forward into late-stage clinical studies. If this candidate fails to deliver a positive outcome in the phase 2a study, then this means the company will have to rely on the development of DT120 ODT for the treatment of patients with MDD and GAD.

Conclusion

Definium Therapeutics is in good shape, as prior testing allowed it to achieve statistical significance in using DT120 ODT for the treatment of patients with GAD. This was both in terms of the HAM-A and MADRS scales over a 12-week period. There is huge potential for this company as it approaches a catalyst-rich period in 2026. There are not only three phase 3 studies that have a data readout relating to DT120 ODT for both MDD and GAD, but there is also the other opportunity with DT402. That is, the use of this other drug candidate to treat the social and communication issues for patients with ASD.

With the progress made to date in advancing several phase 3 studies with DT120 ODT since I last wrote about this company, plus the potential to expand to its other drug candidate DT402, I believe that it is important to maintain a Strong Buy rating on this stock.
